title: Hargreaves Lansdown Apologizes After Major IT Outage Locks Out Thousands
description: Britain's largest investment platform suffered widespread technical failures, leaving customers unable to access accounts or trade.
lede: Hargreaves Lansdown, Britain's biggest investment platform, has resolved a major IT outage that prevented thousands of customers from accessing their accounts. The finance firm issued an apology after its website and mobile app experienced widespread disruption, sparking anger among investors unable to monitor portfolios or execute trades.

sections.heading: Technical Failures Spark Customer Outrage
paragraphs: The outage began early Monday morning, with customers reporting they were unable to log into their accounts through both the website and mobile application. Social media filled with complaints from frustrated investors who found themselves locked out during active trading hours.

Hargreaves Lansdown acknowledged the issues within hours, stating that their technical teams were working urgently to restore full service. The platform, which manages over £140 billion in client assets, serves more than 1.8 million customers across the UK.
Many users expressed particular concern about being unable to monitor their investments during volatile market conditions, with some reporting missed trading opportunities due to the system failures.

sections.heading: Service Restoration and Company Response
paragraphs: By late afternoon, Hargreaves Lansdown confirmed that all systems had been restored to full functionality. The company's technical teams worked through the day to identify and resolve the underlying infrastructure issues that caused the widespread disruption.

Chief Executive Dan Olley issued a formal apology, acknowledging the frustration and inconvenience caused to customers. The company emphasized that no client data was compromised during the technical difficulties.
A spokesperson confirmed that all pending transactions would be processed retroactively and that customers would not incur additional charges for any delayed trades caused by the outage.

sections.heading: Impact on Investment Platform Market
paragraphs: The outage highlights the critical importance of robust IT infrastructure for investment platforms, especially as more retail investors manage portfolios digitally. Hargreaves Lansdown competes with platforms like AJ Bell, Interactive Investor, and newer fintech challengers.

Industry analysts noted that technical reliability has become a key differentiator in the competitive investment platform market, where customers expect 24/7 access to their portfolios and real-time trading capabilities.

The incident comes at a time when retail investment activity remains elevated, with many customers actively managing diversified portfolios including stocks, funds, and ISAs through digital platforms.

sections.heading: Customer Compensation and Future Measures
paragraphs: Hargreaves Lansdown announced it would review all customer accounts affected by the outage to ensure no financial losses occurred due to the service disruption. The company pledged to compensate any customers who suffered demonstrable losses from missed trading opportunities.

The firm committed to investing further in its technology infrastructure to prevent similar incidents in the future. This includes upgrading server capacity and implementing additional backup systems to maintain service continuity.
Customer service teams have been instructed to prioritize calls from clients affected by the outage, with extended support hours implemented to handle the increased volume of inquiries.

sections.heading: Regulatory Scrutiny and Industry Standards
paragraphs: The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has been notified of the incident as part of standard regulatory reporting requirements for significant operational disruptions at major financial services firms.

Industry regulations require investment platforms to maintain adequate business continuity plans and report any material service interruptions that could impact customer outcomes or market confidence.

This incident may prompt broader discussions about operational resilience standards for retail investment platforms, particularly given their growing importance in the UK's retail investment landscape.

sections.heading: Market Position and Recovery Outlook
paragraphs: Despite the temporary setback, Hargreaves Lansdown remains the UK's dominant retail investment platform by assets under management and customer numbers. The company's share price showed minimal impact from the outage news.

Investment analysts view the incident as a operational hiccup rather than a fundamental business issue, noting the company's generally strong track record of service reliability and customer satisfaction.
The swift resolution and transparent communication during the crisis demonstrated the company's commitment to maintaining customer trust and regulatory compliance in a competitive market environment.

faqs.q: What caused the Hargreaves Lansdown outage?
faqs.a: The company experienced widespread IT system failures affecting both their website and mobile app, preventing customer access to accounts and trading services.

faqs.q: How long did the Hargreaves Lansdown outage last?
faqs.a: The outage began early Monday morning and was fully resolved by late afternoon the same day, with services restored to all affected customers.

faqs.q: Will Hargreaves Lansdown compensate customers for the outage?
faqs.a: Yes, the company has committed to reviewing all affected accounts and compensating customers who suffered demonstrable financial losses due to missed trading opportunities.

faqs.q: Is customer data safe after the Hargreaves Lansdown IT issues?
faqs.a: The company confirmed that no client data was compromised during the technical difficulties, and all customer information remains secure.

faqs.q: What is Hargreaves Lansdown doing to prevent future outages?
faqs.a: The firm has committed to additional investment in technology infrastructure, including upgraded server capacity and enhanced backup systems for improved service continuity.
